title: Gonk-a-Bona and the Soup-Seeking Mole
summary: Mickey and Minnie finish a delightful lunch and follow a mysterious humming sound outside to find their friend Mole. Mole has just returned from the grocery store with an eclectic list of soup ingredients, including jalapeños, gouda cheese, and kale. The friends retreat to their shared family house for playtime with trucks before discovering the sleepy Uncle Bill. They show off a magnificent, multi-functional robot named Gonk-a-Bona, which can transform into a lion and travel from the depths of the ocean to the far reaches of outer space. The adventure concludes as the friends settle down to sleep under a canopy of homemade stars.

highlightQuote.text: I was shopping for some carrots, jalapeños, and mushrooms, and gouda cheese, and a bit of extra kale, and some apples for dinner, and some tomatoes, and a little bit of parsley, and a little bit of radish slices.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stories, Stars and Stories it says.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I'll press the Stars and Stories button. Boop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's not the Stars and Stories button, these!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, oh. Boop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stars and Stories, Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's do Stars and Stories. I'll turn on my flashlight and get the basket with the stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I found something even better for stars. We can make mouse stars with one of these sheets, or we can make stars with this blanket. Which would you rather choose?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The blanket, please. This one? Okay, let's test it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We take the blanket... but the blanket has to be up above the light a little bit for it to make stars. If you hold it up here, that makes stars on the ceiling.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So we need to find something that can hold it up. Maybe these two trucks. If I put one truck here and one truck there, and then you put the blanket over them...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dada, I found something different. How about the blanket on top of this? I don't know if that'll work, let's try it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it did change how the stars look. I like that actually. Yes! Now we can tell our stories. Stars and stories...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want a story about just Mickey and Minnie and they don't know where Mole is.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Mickey and Minnie had just finished eating lunch and Mickey said, 'Ha! That was such a nice lunch.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mmm-hmm, mmm-hmm, mmm-hmm... that was Mole's hum.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I thought I heard something.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That was Mole's hum. She was walking around while singing her hum. Mmm-hmm, mmm-hmm...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Minnie said, 'Did you hear that? That was Mole's hum!' And Mickey said, 'Oh, I was wondering where Mole was. Let's follow the hum.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's follow the hum! Mmm-hmm, mmm-hmm...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did they see outside?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they saw Mole! And they said, 'Hello Mole, what you doing?' 'I was just humming while I was walking around in the grocery store and shopping.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what were you shopping for?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I was shopping for some carrots, jalapeños, and mushrooms, and gouda cheese, and a bit of extra kale, and some apples for dinner, and some tomatoes, and a little bit of parsley, and a little bit of radish slices.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow! And Minnie said, 'Those sound like delicious ingredients. What are you going to cook with them?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We can make a bit of vegetable soup! I brought all of the ingredients for your lunch. But first we could just go home and have a bit of playtime and do some things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Playtime? Okay, that's a good idea. And so they all went... whose house did they go to? Mole's house or Mickey and Minnie's house?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A family house! They all live together in one house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They all live together in one house. And so they went... how about we have Stars and Stories playtime together?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, let's have Stars and Stories playtime together. That's where we play around with our trucks and cars and stuff that we have.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they played around with their trucks and cars. But then Mole said, 'Yawn... I'm getting kind of sleepy. I think we should tuck in our toys and tell a story.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Is it time for Stars and Stories? And Mickey said, 'Yeah wow! Okay, I'll tuck in this robot. You tuck in that truck, please.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to tuck in my truck. Brum-brum-brum! How about Mole goes to bed early today and you keep playing, and then you go to bed early today like Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they're all going to go to bed early? And we can have the Stars and Stories playtime. But Mickey said, 'But wait! Before I go to bed, I'd like to hear a little story.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Would you tell me a story, Izzy kiddo? Maybe you could tell me a story about a truck, or even about two trucks.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know if I have any truck stories. Well then once trucks went under the ocean...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, let's do our Stars and Stories family playtime. We're already doing it. Oh, Dad! I found Uncle Bill!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You did! Finally, he was in there the whole time. Uncle Bill, what were you doing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I was just taking a nap. Hey Uncle Bill, look at the robot we built.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that's an incredibly tall robot. I like it. What does it do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It just talks and stuff. Now I see some buttons. What do the buttons do? I don't know. One of three of them just stars a stories and those are the walking buttons, talking buttons, and some buttons on the top that say go and stop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what happens if I press the go button? Can I press it? Boop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Go-go-go, go-go-go, go-go-go!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that's good going. Okay, I'll press stop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stop-stop-stop, stop-stop-stop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now, what happens if I press the Stars and Stories button?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It just says: Stars and stories, stars and stories! Wow, I liked that. I want to press it again. Beep! Stars and stories! And the family stars and stories button says: Stars and stories for families, stars and stories for families!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, I like that one. Wait, now what do the other buttons do? Oh, is there a walking button?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Boop! Walk-walk-walk-walk. And the talking button is this one: Talk-talk-talk. And the singing button: Sing-a-sing-a-sing, sing-a-sing-a-sing.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And it can change into different things. I'll show you how. You just say 'too-loo-loo, too-loo-loo, too-loo-loo, too-loo-loo.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, did that make the robot change into something? What did it change into?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A lion! Roar-roar-roar-roar! 'T-D-wheel-ooh-ana.' That changes it back into a robot.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Cool! That's an amazing robot that you built, it has so many different things it can do when you press the buttons.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And it even has some mission to go underwater. Whoosh! Now it can change out of its dive suit and then it can go in space. Whoosh!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Into space! And then it can take off its space suit and land with a thump.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, you did everything robot. You went deep underwater, you went out into outer space, and then you landed again. Now what's this incredible robot's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Gonk-a-Bona! And we made new stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We did make new stars. Let's... okay, Uncle Bill, me and Izzy are going to lie down on our backs with you to look up at the stars. Wow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I like the combination stars that we made. It's like the stars from the basket, but it's also like the stars from the blanket.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And tomorrow... before nap... we could do some mouse stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mouse stars, yeah, let's do that. But for now I think we better turn off the stars because I think it's probably time for bed.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

characters.name: Minnie
characters.description: A wise veteran of briny depths and interstellar frontiers, Mickey’s resourceful mouse companion and confidant to the Princess of Mouseland guides Izzy and Hattie with a glowing lantern and ingenious Mystery Mousketools. Having recently breathed life into a towering guardian of interlocking bricks, this intrepid traveler now points the way toward the mountain’s peak, pausing to investigate the savory aroma of bacon-scented mushrooms while serving as a compassionate friend whose seasoned instincts help decipher Frasgle’s complex roars and sounds.

characters.name: Mole
characters.description: A resourceful, perpetually humming mole with an impossibly long name, this polite and intrepid mariner serves as a bridge between worlds, translating Frasgle’s unique tongue and guiding the way to the hidden Underground Library. Whether managing rocket controls with wide-eyed curiosity, unraveling treehouse mysteries, or sharing the magic of special Legos, he encourages his companions to embrace the unknown between tending his dandelion yard and baking fragrant banana bread. A lover of both wriggly worms and sweet blueberries, he sings whimsical songs of carnabee sharks and drives a noisy dark brown car, remaining firmly hesitant about boarding giant robots even as he coaxes his friends toward bold new adventures.

characters.name: Uncle Bill
characters.description: A diligent crew member and master Lego architect, this former visitor to Balloon World crafts perfectly symmetrical stairways and shapes iridescent purple slime into glistening rocket ships. Renowned for a sharp eye that once pierced Pluto’s crystalline shadows, they recently turned their gaze toward the forest’s depths, acting as a compassionate friend to discover and tend to a sick creature hidden in the brush. Whether providing blocks for builders, weaving bedtime stories, or offering woodland mercy, they remain a helpful soul. Yet, between these moments of structural precision and quiet rescue, they harbor a penchant for deep rest, often found napping peacefully inside the colorful confines of a toy container, tucked away amidst the playthings they share.

characters.name: Gonk-a-Bona
characters.description: An incredibly tall robot with many buttons that can walk, talk, sing, and transform into a lion.

places.name: Grocery store
places.description: The place where Mole went shopping for carrots, jalapeños, and gouda cheese.

places.name: Family house
places.description: A shared home where Mickey, Minnie, and Mole all live together.

places.name: Under the ocean
places.description: A destination where trucks go in a story, and where the robot goes on a mission.

places.name: Space
places.description: The weightless, black void beyond Earth’s atmosphere is a vast expanse of drifting giant rocks that serves as Captain Izzy’s silent laboratory. Now the final destination of the robot's mission, this lethal frontier requires a specialized suit to navigate its airless and crushing stillness.
